steps: Express internal force as function of position
Set up the integration for displacement at C
question_text:
A prismatic bar $AB$ of length $L$, cross-sectional area $A$, modulus of elasticity $E$, and weight $W$ hangs vertically under its own weight (see figure).
Part (a): Derive a formula for the downward displacement $\delta_{C}$ of point $C$, located at distance $h$ from the lower end of the bar.

history.says: Check the expression you wrote — does it represent the internal axial force $N(y)$ varying with $y$?
history.speaks: hmm that stress expression looks off

history.says: Does the internal force go to zero at the lower end where y=0?
What value does your force expression give at $y=0$?
history.speaks: nah check that force at the bottom

history.says: Does the integral you wrote represent the elongation of segment AC only?
history.speaks: hmm that integral limits look off

history.says: What effect does using 0 as the lower limit have on the displacement you’re calculating?
Which portion of the bar should the integral cover to obtain the displacement at point C?
